chart_or_ui_evidence: summary cards; insight deck; project question
safe_wording_boundary: Describe the dataset as a curated Test-of-Time award sample enriched with public metadata; do not claim complete coverage of all influential CS papers.
remaining_human_check: Confirm final title/subtitle and dataset count in the submitted report.

chart_or_ui_evidence: lag histogram; publication-to-award timeline; time claim cards
report_section: Findings 1: Time scale and recognition lag
safe_wording_boundary: Recognition lag equals announcement year minus publication year; use it as award-timing metadata, not causal evidence that the award changed citations.
remaining_human_check: Verify 2-3 selected paper award citations and keep pre/post citation language observational.

chart_or_ui_evidence: top venue bars; venue area bars; venue-decade heatmap; venue claim cards
safe_wording_boundary: Venue counts describe visible award-history coverage in this dataset; do not frame them as conference quality rankings.
remaining_human_check: Choose 2-3 venue examples for slides and verify their source URLs before strong case wording.

chart_or_ui_evidence: topic distribution; topic evolution chart; representative paper detail; topic claim cards
report_section: Findings 3: Topic patterns and research archetypes
safe_wording_boundary: Topic labels combine API metadata and manual audit; treat corrected/manual labels as presentation candidates and mark uncertain labels as needing review.
remaining_human_check: Open evidence URLs for final representative papers and adjust contribution wording if human reading disagrees with API topic.

chart_or_ui_evidence: citation vs lag scatter; trajectory chart; depth-breadth scatter; quadrant cards; citation claim cards
report_section: Findings 4: Citation depth, trajectory, and breadth
safe_wording_boundary: Citation depth and impact breadth are OpenAlex-derived descriptive proxies, not complete citation graphs or official award reasons.
remaining_human_check: Use coverage matrix statuses to decide which representative cases are presentation-ready and which need human checks.

chart_or_ui_evidence: searchable evidence index; sorted paper list; detail-linked selected paper
safe_wording_boundary: Explorer rows are a navigation aid over the dataset; do not treat missing manual annotations as negative evidence about a paper.
remaining_human_check: For final report cases, prefer rows marked presentation-ready-cautious and keep unchecked cases in backup.

chart_or_ui_evidence: selected-paper percentile comparison; field median comparison; benchmark story card
safe_wording_boundary: Percentiles are within the current enriched dataset or same-field subset, not universal CS-wide rankings.
remaining_human_check: Confirm chosen benchmark paper and avoid overclaiming when field subset size is small.

safe_wording_boundary: Story Builder is a report-planning crosswalk; final prose still needs owner review and evidence URL checks.
remaining_human_check: Each owner should copy only the claim rows they verified into the final report.

chart_or_ui_evidence: metric cards; safe wording phrases; proxy definitions
safe_wording_boundary: Use glossary definitions consistently; do not replace metric boundaries with stronger causal or official-judgment language.
remaining_human_check: Paste the final wording into PPT speaker notes and keep terms consistent across slides.

chart_or_ui_evidence: institution bars; country bars; network KPIs; network claim cards
safe_wording_boundary: Institution and country views reflect affiliation metadata visibility in OpenAlex-derived records; avoid ranking institutions or countries by research quality.
remaining_human_check: Select final network cases and preserve metadata-boundary wording during presentation rehearsal.
